title: [Bug]: Podman Machine Init fails with Error: exit status 1
body:
### Issue Description

I am attempting to run a podman virtual machine in WSL2 to experiment with aarch64 emulation. I had some success with the version of podman that Ubuntu 22 has, but podman 3.x did not have volume mounting in the virtual machine. Now that I have upgraded to podman `4.3.1`, I am unable to start the virtual machine. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. create new ubuntu WSL2 instance
2. install podman 4.3.1
3. `sudo mount --make-rshared /`
4. `sudo apt install qemu-system qemu-user-static`
5. install gvproxy
6. `sudo chown root:kvm /dev/kvm && sudo chmod 660 /dev/kvm`
7. `sudo usermod -aG kvm $USER`
8. logout and back in
9. podman machine init


### Describe the results you received

```
Extracting compressed file
Image resized.
Error: exit status 1
```
turning up `--log-level=debug` yields nothing new or useful. 

Only `~/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.json` is created, not the `ign` file or any others. 

### Describe the results you expected

Having the machine initialize correctly.

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Additional information

comments.body:
Are you just wanting to run aarch container images on x86? If so you shouldn't need to do a nested virt podman machine inside of wsl. 

These two options should work if that's the case:

1. Install podman for winodws, letting it create its own WSL instance it uses
    a. From there you can podman machine ssh into the WSL instance (or access it via wsl -d podman-machine-default) and install qemu-user-static
2. Setup a custom WSL dristo like you are doing with podman installed, and then install qemu-user-static however your distro likes. 

The advantage of 1) is that podman can be used from Windows,  and the Fedora streams usually stay more current with the podman backend piece.

@n1hility thank you for the information. I am attempting what you said, and had switched distros in the middle of my testing. On the first distro, I was unable to get 2) working but more than likely was a PEBCAK. This step did work on my current test, so I am able to continue working. Thank you for the information!

Feel free to close this issue as I can continue on, or keep it open if you want to run the init issue to ground.
Cool thanks @thejtshow . I'll close it for now, but planning to include aarch qemu packages by default in the future, so that this use case will work out of the box for the podman machine case.
